scope: Verified repeat-failure auto-escalation / subagent-spawning behavior for Ralph docs watchdog framework, runtime script, and live cron presence.
findings: The framework doc was already broadly aligned with aggressive repeat-failure handling, but it did not explicitly require the live watchdog prompt/runtime to treat persistent repeat failure as an all-hands subagent-spawning event.
The live watchdog runtime previously reran the local runner only once and had no persistent runtime memory for consecutive failures or same-fingerprint repeat failure detection.

The live crontab currently shows only comment lines for the Ralph docs watchdog runner / independent verifier near lines 15-16; no active cron command for job c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80 was found in the accessible crontab output.

An existing docs_stack_parallel_signoff.json currently claims deactivation approval, but that alone is not enough proof that live repeat-failure auto-escalation will trigger all-hands behavior automatically.
repairsApplied: Patched agents/docs_quality/docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py to add repeat-failure runtime state, consecutive-failure tracking, same-fingerprint failure detection, stronger aggressive repair passes, and explicit REPEAT_FAILURE_ESCALATION_REQUIRED / AUTO-ESCALATION RULE reporting in failure reasons.

Patched agents/docs_quality/REPEAT_FAILURE_RESPONSE_FRAMEWORK.md to explicitly require all-hands watchdog/runtime escalation with parallel subagent spawning when repeat failure persists past ordinary repair attempts.
Patched agents/docs_quality/README.md to state that repeat-failure auto-escalation into all-hands repair and parallel subagent spawning is required before aggressive self-heal deactivation.
proof: python3 -m py_compile succeeded for agents/docs_quality/docs_stack_temp_watchdog.py, agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_runner.py, and agents/docs_quality/ralph_docs_verify.py.
The patched watchdog source now contains runtime state persistence (docs_stack_temp_watchdog_runtime.json), repeat failure thresholds, and explicit escalation reason emission.
crontab inspection returned docs comment markers but no active accessible command tied to the requested cron id, so live automatic execution strength could not be confirmed.
blockers: Live runtime approval is withheld because the accessible crontab output did not show an active watchdog/verifier command for the named cron job id c99cfa5f-41a1-4bc2-845f-6b6e1d5c7e80.
I did not approve because 'should trigger all-hands automatically' requires not just stronger code/prompts but confirmed live scheduling / invocation wiring.
